summary: Crisp soundboard capture of a legendary Missoula ’74 show highlighted by a smoking WRS > Dark Star > China Doll and scorching Scarlet Begonias.
review: The recording is a clean, well‑balanced soundboard tape that lets every instrument shine – from Keith’s soaring keys to Bill’s thunderous drums. Musically the concert is often called one of the year’s best, with a second‑set jam marathon (WRS → Dark Star → China Doll, Playing in the Band, Not Fade Away → GDTRFB) that fans repeatedly cite as unforgettable. A few reviewers note occasional vocal quirks or mix quirks, but they are far outweighed by the overwhelming praise for the band’s inspired playing.

ai_rating.rationale: The vast majority of reviews (≈90 %) award 4–5 stars and extol both the musical performance and the audio fidelity; only a handful of outliers criticize mix balance or vocals, so the combined show‑quality (70 %) and recording quality (30 %) yields a rating in the high‑4 range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Consistently described as crisp, clear, and well‑balanced; occasional comments about a weak Jerry mix are isolated and contradicted by many who say the guitar is front‑and‑center.

standout_songs: Wharf Rat Suite > Dark Star > China Doll
Not Fade Away > Good Times Bad Times (GDTRFB)
poor_songs: Donna vocal sections (some reviewers note mis‑cues)
show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is described as solid and spartan, while the second set delivers a seamless jam flow with stellar transitions (WRS → Dark Star → China Doll, then into NFA → GDTRFB). Overall pacing and energy are praised as high‑octane throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly lauded for inspired, soaring guitar work; a single reviewer claims his mix is weak, but most say he’s front‑and‑center and “hammering away.”
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for solid low‑frequency bass presence and overall tight playing.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for strong rhythm guitar and vocals; also mentioned being struck by a plastic pitcher during the show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Godchaux receives high marks for his piano/moog work, “shining” especially on early Scarlet and improvisational passages.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming is described as “on fire,” “destroying those drums,” with particular acclaim for the intense Playing in the Band section.
